title: Add User model with bcrypt password hashing
description: Create a new User model file at `src/models/user.model.ts` with fields: `id` (uuid), `email` (unique/not null), `passwordHash` (not null), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`. Implement bcrypt hashing with cost factor 12. Include a class with methods for creating users, verifying passwords, and managing the user store. Add unit tests covering: user creation, password hash verification, edge cases (empty email, invalid email format, duplicate email).
existing_patterns: src/auth/user-store.ts - UserStore class pattern with Map-based storage, password hashing, and CRUD methods
src/models/notification.ts - model file location and JSDoc comment convention
src/auth/user-store.test.ts - describe/it vitest testing patterns with beforeEach setup
questions: Should bcryptjs be added as a direct dependency, or should Node.js crypto.scrypt be used as a bcrypt-compatible alternative (since no bcrypt library is currently in package.json)?
Since the existing UserStore uses SHA-256 and AuthService uses PBKDF2, should this new User model be independent or share validation logic with those systems?
What email validation format should be enforced - simple presence check or RFC-compliant email regex?
